top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $750] Audio Note Recording
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build full audio-note recording, playback, transcription, and management support for the warpSpeed Notes experience. This bounty allows users to record quick voice notes directly inside the app, save them as audio attachments to notes, transcribe them into text, and replay/manage them later through a reliable in-note audio player. The implementation should use the provided app skeleton and OpenAPI spec, with clean separation between UI components, audio logic, backend helpers,

top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $660] Inline Image Editing
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build an inline image editing feature for the warpSpeed app. This bounty allows users to make quick, lightweight image edits directly inside the app without exporting images to another tool. From the image preview, users should be able to open an editor, crop, rotate, adjust basic image settings, and add simple annotations. Edited images must be saved as a new version while keeping the original image intact. This bounty is part of the warpSpeed OPEN developer bounty programme.

top_opportunities.title: Use JuliaLowering.jl for a cleaner way to transform resumable functions to finite state machines [$600]
top_opportunities.body_preview: Edit: #100 has fixed most of these issues without relying on the not-yet-published JuliaLowering.jl. It uses a local reimplementation of most scoping rules of the language, thus missing edge cases might exist. Leaving this (now smaller) bounty for future work of moving this implementation to JuliaLowering, to provide a more centralized source-of-truth on scoping. --- This software transform annotated julia functions into finite state machines that can be paused and re-entered. top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Adding Privacy to an Existing dApp: A Retrofit Guide

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Shielded Token Operations: Mint, Transfer & Burn with Test Suite
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Walk through the complete shielded token lifecycle in Compact: minting, transferring, and burning, with compilable code and a full test suite. No UI required, this is focused on the contract and testing layer. Covers: - Minting with `mintShieldedToken` and `evolveNonce` - Transferring with `sendShielded` and change management via `ShieldedSendResult` - Burning via `sendImmediateShielded` to `shieldedBurnAddress()` - The Merkle tree constraint (freshly m

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Replay Attack Prevention in Compact: Nonces, Nullifiers & Domain Separation
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Explain three replay prevention mechanisms: (1) Counter-based nonces that increment per operation, (2) Set-based nullifiers derived from `persistentCommit(secret, context)`, (3) Domain separation tags in hash functions to prevent cross-circuit replay. Show working examples of each and when to use which pattern. ## Target Audience Developers ## Deliverables 1. **Written tutorial (2,500-3,500 words)** covering: - Counter-based nonces that increment per op

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Time and Deadlines in Compact: Block Time, Counters & the Uint<16> Problem

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Midnight vs Other Privacy Chains: Architecture Comparison for Developers
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Compare Midnight's architecture (Compact + ZK circuits + Zswap + dual ledger) with other privacy-focused chains (Aztec's Noir + UTXO, Aleo's Leo + records, Mina's o1js + recursive SNARKs, Zcash's Sapling). Focus on developer experience: language design, state model, privacy model. top_opportunities.title: [Feature] Limit Order Execution via Off-Chain Keepers
top_opportunities.body_preview: **Description:** Currently, TradeFlow only supports market orders. We want to allow users to place limit orders. The core contract must store the order intent, allowing off-chain "keepers" to execute the trade when the market price meets the order criteria. **Context / Motivation:** Advanced traders require limit orders. Moving this on-chain allows TradeFlow to capture a more sophisticated demographic of DeFi users while rewarding keepers for network maintenance. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] I
